etymology.description: The phrase 我知道 (wǒ zhīdào, "I know") combines the pronoun for oneself with the act of comprehension or awareness.
etymology.interpretation: 我(我) (wǒ, "I") is the pronoun used to express the self. 知(知) (zhī, "know") indicates awareness or knowledge, while 道(道) (dào, "way; path; to say") suggests a path or method of understanding, as well as verbal expression. Together, 知道 (zhīdào, "know") conveys the action of understanding or having knowledge of something, with 我(我) (wǒ, "I") making it personal, resulting in the meaning "I know."

examples.chinese: 我知道他在图书馆(我知道他在圖書館)。
examples.english: I know he is in the library.

examples.chinese: 他告诉我一个秘密(他告訴我一個祕密)，我知道了(我知道了)。
examples.pinyin: Tā gàosù wǒ yī gè mìmì, wǒ zhīdào le.
examples.english: He told me a secret, I know now.

examples.chinese: 我知道她今天会来(我知道她今天會來)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zhīdào tā jīntiān huì lái.
examples.english: I know she will come today.
